Title: The B3 domain-containing transcription factor ZmABI19 coordinates expression of key factors required for maize seed development and grain filling

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in BETL at early seed stage
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq target identification; DLR transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct target of ZmABI19 likely promoting nutrient transport and endosperm filling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWEET4c is directly bound and transactivated by ZmABI19. 2. It functions in nutrient import across the BETL. 3. This links ZmABI19 to sink strength and grain filling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve assimilate import and kernel filling, especially together with regulatory network engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Considered a master regulator for BETL cell fate in cited background literature
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SWEET4c promoter tested in DLR assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nutrient allocation to developing seed

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq target identification; DLR transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct ZmABI19 target implicated in auxin-responsive seed development that supports grain filling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF4 promoter is directly transactivated by ZmABI19. 2. ARF4 belongs to the auxin-response branch altered in zmabi19. 3. It supports the role of ZmABI19 in early hormone-regulated seed development.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for tuning early seed development, but metabolism effect is indirect
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly validated as a ZmABI19 target in hormone signaling, though not a direct metabolic enzyme/regulator of nutrient branch
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ARF4 promoter in DLR assay

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq target identification; DLR transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct ZmABI19 target implicated in auxin-responsive seed development that supports grain filling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF26 promoter is directly transactivated by ZmABI19. 2. ARF26 is part of the auxin-responsive module altered in zmabi19. 3. It supports ZmABI19 control of early seed developmental programs tied to nutrient accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential developmental engineering node, with indirect effects on nutrient deposition
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ARF26 promoter in DLR assay

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of multiple grain-filling regulators and transporter genes; coordinates expression of seed development regulators; can be antagonized by O11 via protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: O2; Pbf1; ZmbZIP22; NAC130; O11; Vp1; SWEET4c; ARF4; ARF26; NAC86; NAC134
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates O2, Pbf1, ZmbZIP22, NAC130, O11, Vp1, SWEET4c and selected auxin-response genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Grain filling initiation; storage protein biosynthesis; starch and sucrose metabolism; lipid metabolism; nutrient transport; embryo and endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA enhances transactivation of Vp1 promoter; auxin-related developmental context inferred from regulated ARF/Aux-IAA genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream hub regulator and grain filling initiation regulator coordinating embryo and endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in developing seeds; peaks at 2 DAP; high in early seed development; later stronger in embryo than endosperm; abundant in basal scutellum and BETL at 16 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in developing seeds; peaks at 2 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase reporter assays; cytology
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function causes opaque, small, flattened, non-germinating kernels with reduced starch, zeins and lipids, increased soluble sugars, severe endosperm and embryo developmental defects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmABI19 directly binds the O2 promoter RY motifs and activates O2 expression. 2. ZmABI19 directly regulates multiple key grain-filling TFs and SWEET4c, forming an upstream regulatory hub. 3. zmabi19 mutants show severe defects in grain filling, endosperm/embryo development, and storage reserve accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve maize grain filling and kernel nutrient accumulation, but knockout is seed lethal; engineering should preserve developmental function and may require dosage/tissue-specific modulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes embryo lethality, severe kernel size reduction, opaque kernels, and broad metabolic defects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and ChIP-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA629420
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 8 DAP and 16 DAP developmental stages; ABA addition enhanced Vp1 promoter transactivation in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserves supporting embryo development, germination, and grain nutritional value
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Initiates O2 expression, after which O2 auto-activates its own promoter; part of a feedforward grain-filling regulatory cascade
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognizes primarily RY motifs, but also binds a G-box in the Vp1 promoter; protein accumulation suggests transcriptional and translational regulation

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; auto-activation of own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Zein genes; PPDK1; PPDK2; SS3; Sh1; Sus1; Sus2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates zein genes and starch/sucrose metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Endosperm filling; storage protein biosynthesis; starch biosynthesis; sucrose cleavage
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated by ZmABI19; repressed antagonistically by O11 in context of ZmABI19-mediated transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional interactions with PBF1; interacts with MADS47; works with OHPs and ZmbZIP22 in zein regulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PBF1; MADS47; OHP1; OHP2; O11; ZmABI19
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator/central regulator of endosperm filling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Begins around 8 DAP and rises rapidly during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used in effector assay; native O2 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Its target zein genes are described as undergoing endosperm activation with removal of silent chromatin marks and DNA methylation; O2 promoter activation is proposed to occur through a similar early-to-late epigenetic transition, but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; mutant expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced O2 expression in zmabi19 is associated with decreased zeins and starch biosynthetic capacity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 is a direct target of ZmABI19 through two RY motifs in its promoter. 2. O2 can auto-activate its own promoter via the O2 box. 3. O2 is a central downstream regulator linking ZmABI19 to zein and starch synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key downstream node for improving endosperm storage reserves; may be combined with upstream regulators for grain quality engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage proteins and starch contribute to kernel nutritional quality and seed vigor
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Autoregulatory positive feedback on own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central in the paper, O2 is mainly a downstream target used to reveal the upstream regulator ZmABI19

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of ZmABI19; synergistic regulation with O2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates grain filling genes, with stronger synergistic activity together with O2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Endosperm storage protein and starch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important endosperm-specific grain-filling regulator coordinated by ZmABI19
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Pbf1 promoter in reporter assay; 35S promoter used for ZmABI19 effector
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated in zmabi19, contributing to reduced zein and starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pbf1 is directly bound by ZmABI19 at an RY motif in its promoter. 2. ZmABI19 activates Pbf1 transcription. 3. Reduced Pbf1 in zmabi19 likely contributes to defective grain filling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for modulating endosperm protein/starch accumulation, potentially with O2-centered engineering
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at grain filling stage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports storage reserve deposition in maize endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of coordinated feedforward grain filling network downstream of ZmABI19
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Milder transactivation alone than O2 but acts synergistically with O2

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates 27-kD γ-zein expression together with O2, PBF1 and OHPs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Works with O2, PBF1 and OHPs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important endosperm-specific grain-filling TF for γ-zein branch
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter tested in DLR assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq target identification; DLR transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted reduction in zmabi19 contributes to lower zein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmbZIP22 is a direct target of ZmABI19. 2. ZmABI19 transactivates the ZmbZIP22 promoter. 3. This provides an upstream link to γ-zein regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target for endosperm storage protein engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage protein component affecting kernel quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional evidence here is promoter transactivation rather than direct phenotyping of zmbzip22 mutants

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with NAC128 in known background pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key endosperm TF linking ZmABI19 to starch and zein synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter tested in DLR assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq target identification; DLR transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to reduced Bt2/16-kD γ-zein expression and lower starch/protein accumulation in zmabi19
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC130 is a direct target of ZmABI19. 2. ZmABI19 transactivates the NAC130 promoter. 3. This helps explain broad reduction of zeins and starch-related genes in zmabi19.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target in multilayer grain quality engineering with upstream regulators
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NAC128 is discussed as partner/background regulator but not directly validated here as ZmABI19 target

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation by ZmABI19; antagonizes ZmABI19-mediated activation of O2 through protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Nkd2; ZmDof3; Pbf1; O2; PPDK genes; multiple carbohydrate metabolic enzyme genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Pbf1 and some metabolic genes; represses O2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-setting regulator that integrates developmental control with nutrient metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-specific; activated as early as 3 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native O11 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; EMSA; DLR assay; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; LCI; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated in zmabi19; contributes to altered endosperm development and grain filling network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O11 is directly bound and activated by ZmABI19 via an RY motif in its promoter. 2. O11 physically interacts with ZmABI19. 3. O11 weakens ZmABI19-mediated transactivation of O2, indicating antagonistic crosstalk within the grain-filling network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important network node for balancing developmental and storage programs; engineering should consider antagonism with ZmABI19 and repression of O2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May repress O2 while activating other targets, implying complex outcomes on storage composition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early seed development and grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to endosperm reserve accumulation and kernel development
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Antagonistic regulatory loop with ZmABI19 on O2 promoter output
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dual activator/repressor properties noted from previous work and supported here

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3 domain transcription factor; ABI3/VP1 homolog
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation by ZmABI19; ABA-enhanced promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Globulin1; Globulin2 and embryo maturation/ABA signaling genes (background from literature)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes embryo storage reserve accumulation and embryogenesis-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Embryo development; storage protein accumulation; oil accumulation; nutrient reallocation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated by ZmABI19; ABA enhances ZmABI19-mediated transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key embryo regulator linking ZmABI19 to lipid/protein accumulation and embryogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Starts 3-5 DAP and peaks around 10 DAP; embryo/scutellum-associated
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Vp1 promoter in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; DLR assay with ABA treatment; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced Vp1 expression in zmabi19 is associated with embryo developmental arrest and reduced lipid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vp1 is a direct target of ZmABI19. 2. ZmABI19 binds a G-box in the Vp1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. ABA enhances ZmABI19-mediated Vp1 activation, linking the network to hormone signaling and embryo storage reserve accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential embryo-targeted engineering node for storage reserve allocation, but manipulation should consider embryo viability and ABA dependence
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA addition in DLR assay; embryo development stage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Embryo reserves support germination and seed quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike most validated targets, ZmABI19 binds a G-box rather than an RY motif in the Vp1 promoter
